programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A charity chairman dies mysteriously, and the prime suspect is then killed at the funeral. The investigation leads to hospital administrator Norman Briggs - who could either be the guilty party or the next victim. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A womanising patient disappears and Dr Sloan finds himself trying to untangle an ambitious murder plot. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble becomes delirious as a result of an accident and reveals his terrible secret to Ruth Norton, a distraught airline stewardess.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman is convinced her friend's husband is trying to kill her after she discovers he is a bigamist, but when he is found dead, she's the prime suspect.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Sydney helps Gage come to terms with his disability, and the Rangers try to track down carjackers. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team tries to clear Alexx of murder, but the task is made difficult by the fact that the victim was a known child molester whose crimes had led to universal revulsion, and whose move to a local neighbourhood the medical examiner had been campaigning against. Drama, starring David Caruso and Khandi Alexander

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Kyle Friedgen is accused of killing his ex-girlfriend, but while his case is dismissed due to a mishandled warrant, his life and reputation are marred by suspicion that he got away with murder. Determined to clear his name in full, Friedgen volunteers to be court-martialed, on the condition that the NCIS team conducts its own investigation. Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble decides to risk a visit home to check on his ailing father.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman learns that her late grandfather in Hong Kong left her a small fortune in diamonds, but when a search for them ends in murder, she turns to Perry for help.

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The Rangers investigate a black market trade and Alex finds herself in danger of being exposed to the Ebola virus. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bank at Quantico is robbed and a security guard killed, but the team soon discovers that stealing money may not have been the perpetrators' primary objective. Meanwhile, Gibbs is given the task of evaluating an inexperienced new agent assigned by Vance to help investigate the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rey Valentin

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The two Marine brothers of a sailor battling leukaemia are killed in the line of duty, and to spare the servicemen's family further grief, the secretary of the US Navy seeks the NCIS team's help in locating a suitable bone marrow donor. Meanwhile, Bishop returns home to Oklahoma for Thanksgiving. Military crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A murder inquiry puts Mac and Stella on the trail of a dangerous gang that kills for sport. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden investigate the death of a woman who worked at an illegal massage parlour. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the death of a circus performer stuffed into a box on a beach, but probing the rivalries and secret love affairs at the big top fails to yield a clue to the killer's identity. Meanwhile, Danny and Flack try to solve the riddle of a pool of blood in an apartment building - and a blocked rubbish chute may provide the answer

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The death of a seemingly happy and healthy bride moments before her wedding leaves Mac and Danny with a complex puzzle, especially as the doves due to be released after the ceremony have also been killed. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden track down the corpse of a man who apparently chewed off his own hand, and struggle to ascertain the significance of a hole next to the body. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members assist Ralph when he unlocks a secret level in a dark website video game, and it unintentionally reveals the location of a CIA safe house. Meanwhile, Walter decides to enter an aerospace contest. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is tasked to discover the identity of a mole who leaked classified information when the CIA's Internal Affairs division intercepts an encrypted message sent by someone in its Los Angeles office. Meanwhile, Paige confronts her feelings for Walter. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A childhood friend of Gibbs' dead daughter is stalked by a marine who recently returned from Iraq, and asks the agent for help. Memories of his murdered family lead him to make the case a priority, but he acts on his initiative and leaves HQ without his badge, making DiNozzo fear he is risking his job for a personal vendetta. Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby babysits a bright nine-year-old while the other members of the team try to locate the youngster's missing father - a survivalist wanted in connection with a case of robbery and murder. The search takes them deep into the wilderness - where a startling discovery awaits. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. Ducky refuses to perform an autopsy out of respect for the man's religion, but Abby runs tests that reveal his demise was actually due to poisoning from a rare octopus venom. Meanwhile, McGee goes into the mosque intending to plant a concealed microphone - but discovers someone has had the same idea

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members assist Ralph when he unlocks a secret level in a dark website video game, and it unintentionally reveals the location of a CIA safe house. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. However, incriminating evidence quickly piles up against him - and a second death complicates matters further. Meanwhile, Ziva lets her hair down to obtain fingerprints from a big oil lobbyist. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs revisits his home town to investigate an assault that left one marine dead and another badly injured, and enlists the help of his estranged father to identify the assailant. Suspicion initially falls on a local man whose daughter was dating the survivor of the attack - but later inquiries uncover a tangled tale of uncertain paternity and forbidden love. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine is found murdered in his garage, and inquiries lead the agents to his former sergeant. However, Gibbs and the team soon find out that their man is under FBI witness protection as he is about to testify against a mobster, giving him a cast-iron alibi. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A serial killer begins posting footage of his crimes online - and as the agents investigate, they realise that each film contains visual clues to the identity of the next victim. In a further bizarre twist, Abby later finds what seems to be a new video on her computer - and the case takes on fresh urgency with the discovery that it is actually a live feed, suggesting the murderer is somewhere on the premises

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison has a dream in which the location of a murder weapon is revealed, but later finds herself called to court to explain how she knew where it would be found. Meanwhile, a fellow psychic warns of trouble in Joe's future. Starring Patricia Arquette and Jake Weber

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Part one of two. A female lieutenant is wrongly given the credit for shooting down two enemy fighters during a combat mission, but is uncomfortable with the plaudits heaped on her. To make matters worse, a male comrade tries to prove he is the better pilot - with tragic consequences. Drama, starring David James Elliott and Katie Rich

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: A drunken woman vanishes in the water forcing the boys to call in the helicopter to help find her. Terry has the most critical rescue of the season, which also brings back some emotional memories for him

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella question the employees of a dock worker whose crushed remains have been found on Staten Island, before being led to another corpse - but suspects for both murders are thin on the ground. Meanwhile, an apparent hit-and-run turns into a complex puzzle for Danny and Aiden. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo and Vanessa Ferlito

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella have reason to suspect a police officer is involved in the murder of a wine store owner after evidence links him to a Hispanic gang present at the crime scene. Their investigations prove upsetting for Flack, as the man under suspicion is his old mentor

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Dan Stanwyck, an investigator working on a report into police corruption, is fatally shot the night before his findings are published. Taylor and Stella are convinced he was killed by a crooked officer whose wrongdoings were about to be exposed. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden look into the case of a taxi driver stabbed to death in his cab

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to investigate the murder of a retired US Navy lieutenant commander, and the inquiry forces Ducky to reveal he has been working as part of a secret society dedicated to solving cold cases. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo is reunited with his old flame, Jeanne Benoit, when insurgents in Sudan attack a group of volunteer doctors, including her husband. Travelling to the crime scene to conduct a joint murder-kidnapping investigation, Dinozzi and McGee offer their assistance in searching for the missing doctors. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray, with Scottie Thompson

programme.desc: CID officers attend the funeral of murdered supermodel Cindy Statham, only to learn that killer Larry Franks has been transferred to hospital following a suicide bid. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry tries to raise morale on the ward following Jim's death and Abs manages to break down Nina's defences when they are forced to treat a patient together. With Simon MacCorkindale, James Redmond and Georgina Bouzova

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Larry Franks withdraws his confession to the murder of supermodel Cindy Statham and Jo reopens the case, convinced they have the wrong man in custody. Crime scene photographer Rachel Inns helps her work late into the night re-examining the evidence. Mickey investigates a smash-and-grab raid on a jewellery shop and ends up arresting an old friend, Mod cafe owner Jerry Harper, whose daughter owns the scooter used as a getaway vehicle

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky wants to get back together with Bianca, but she'll have to think about it. Pauline organises a funeral and Alan proposes to Carol

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo, Alvin and Entwistle set out to save the day when Howard suspects Pearl is seeing someone else and the trio soon draw up a surveillance operation that leads them to a seedy bikers' club. Toby sets out to find Aunty Wainwright when he has ironing problems

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After suffering a brush with Stella, Hobbo comes to the conclusion that it is time to find her a man - and, with the help of Clegg and Truly, he decides that eligible village heart-throb Norris Fairburn is the right match. Pearl's new-found passion for motorcycling causes trouble for Howard and Marina

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: An arsonist terrorises a housing estate, and Hetty goes undercover in a quilt-making circle in her search for the culprit, while Robert is confined at home with a broken collarbone and has to content himself with Polaroid shots of his burgeoning runner bean crop

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Fantasy drama, starring John Simm as a detective who ends up in a coma while pursuing a serial killer - and when he comes round, he finds he has somehow been transported back to 1973. As he attempts to uncover what has happened to him, he must also contend with the macho behaviour of his Sweeneyesque colleagues, as well as a murderer whose crimes are oddly similar to the one he was investigating in his own time

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A reluctant Brian enters an addiction centre run by monks to treat his alcoholism, but soon becomes bored and looks for a distraction. He finds one when a chance remark leads the team to reopen an investigation into the death of a heroin user nine years previously - and to help reveal the truth, Gerry is sent undercover as a sex addict. Richard Wilson and Fay Ripley guest star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team is called in to investigate when a convicted killer is cleared of the murder of his adoptive father on appeal. Not fully convinced the man is innocent, Boyd tries to find the rest of the family to establish the truth, but when he does eventually track the relatives down, he realises they have a lot to hide. Drama, starring Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston and Claire Goose

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Henry is left heartbroken when Jess falls for a hunky waiter, and the members of the nursing team help a young couple who are being cruelly manipulated by a selfish old man as they attempt to cope with a life-changing dilemma. Meanwhile, Anna is preoccupied with a problem of her own, and Simon is not the only one to fall victim to her sharp tongue. Drama, starring Lesley Dunlop, Andrew Knott and Kelly Wenham

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Learning Napoleon has returned from exile, Sharpe leaves Lucille to look after their farm and returns to service on the Prince of Orange's staff. It isn't long before he's reunited with the remaining Chosen Men for one of the most momentous military engagements of all time - the Battle of Waterloo. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and Cecile Paoli star

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Dennis does his bit to cement Anglo-German relations by taking Dagmar for a quiet weekend away. Unfortunately for him, Wayne and Barry blunder in at the crucial moment and reduce his planned seduction to a building-site get-together

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads encounter a fellow Brit while spending a relaxing weekend in the country - but his hunger, exhaustion and evasive behaviour arouse their suspicions. Ray Winstone guests alongside regulars Jimmy Nail, Kevin Whately and Timothy Spall

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads are forced to take a well-known British trouble-maker under their collective wing, but his presence in the hut soon leads to friction and a beating for Neville. Michael Elphick guest stars in the popular comedy drama

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary investigate the mysterious death of a blind professor caught up in a bitter battle to save his beloved allotment from an unscrupulous land developer. A heady blend of weedkiller, cannabis and high finance hangs in the air as the duo search for clues - and another body is turned up before they track down the villain. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura are employed to restore a country churchyard in time for a medieval fair, but it is not long before a local landowner is killed by an arrow beneath an ancient yew in the centre of the graveyard. The duo investigate the possibility that rival bidders for the victim's highly desirable farm are to blame - though affairs of the heart might offer a more likely motive. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Jared discovers a severed human hand in a crayfish pot, begging the questions who does it belong to and are they still alive? Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: A hotel concierge falls to his death with Phryne's stolen bag and the trail of clues leads to Baron Henry Fisher - the sleuth's eccentric father

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina risks her career to prove Donald is innocent when a woman is attacked in Victoria Park. Meanwhile, Jack uncovers important information in connection with the case of the missing boys, while Pratt experiences problems with a boy of his own. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Jennie Jacques

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Craggy cop Jim Taggart investigates the murder of an undertaker found in a burnt-out car, and soon realises that all the evidence points to local French teacher Francoise Campbell, whose pupils seem strangely anxious to protect her. Whodunit, starring Mark McManus, James MacPherson, Iain Anders and Robert Robertson

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Two skulls are found during the construction of a new bypass, and the detective investigates a possible link to the theft of poisonous snakes from a Glasgow research laboratory. Drama, starring Mark McManus, Blythe Duff and Dougray Scott

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

Following a shoot-out with the East Street gang, he is ordered to act as courier when a hostage is exchanged for a gun the Skens are eager to get their hands on - and finds he is in danger from both sides. Back at the station, Mickey and Kezia work on a case in which explosives have gone missing from a contractor's depot. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the tense conclusion of the two-part story, Heaton briefs the team on the Skens' plan to ambush a cash van, but Lewis alerts them to a last-minute change of plan - the stolen explosives are to be used to rescue gang leader Wayne Jackson from a prison vehicle on the courthouse run. A high-speed car chase through the London streets ensues, observed from the air by Jack until the getaway car enters a tunnel. The trail goes cold, leaving Lewis in the hands of the increasingly suspicious gangsters

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Darren Day guest stars as a photographer arrested on suspicion of murdering one of his models. The discovery of a blood-soaked T-shirt at his trashed studio, and the evidence of his own pictures, suggests a second suspect may have delivered the fatal blows to the victim's head. Emma and Beth tangle with a trio of truculent young women whose troubled history of deceit and blackmail leads to violence, putting the young PCs in danger

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CID officers attend the funeral of murdered supermodel Cindy Statham, only to learn that killer Larry Franks has been transferred to hospital following a suicide bid. programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning writer Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel, first published in 1815. Romola Garai stars as the independent Emma Woodhouse, who believes she is a skilled matchmaker and tries to pair up her friends and acquaintances. She takes Harriet Smith under her wing and encourages the woman to set her sights higher than her current suitor - against Mr Knightley's warnings about the dangers of meddling. With Jonny Lee Miller, Michael Gambon, Jodhi May and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: While continuing her efforts to set up Harriet and the vicar, Emma looks forward to meeting Frank Churchill at the Christmas party, but when he fails to show up she becomes the subject of Mr Elton's unwanted attention. Later, village gossip focuses on the arrival of Jane Fairfax and a piano she was given by a mystery admirer, leaving the matchmaker reluctant to believe Mr Knightley could have sent the gift. Drama, starring Romola Garai, Louise Dylan, Blake Ritson and Laura Pyper

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The heroine plans a ball with Frank, and wonders whether she has feelings for him. Despite having a wonderful time at the event, she still feels trapped, and with the matchmaking effort officially abandoned, becomes increasingly bored. However, her spirits are raised by Mr Knightley's proposal for a group outing to Box Hill, where he tells her he thinks Frank and Jane are in love. Drama, starring Romola Garai and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The outing to Box Hill ends in disaster when Emma insults Miss Bates. After being berated by Knightley, she realises her behaviour was shameful and tries to make amends, but Jane refuses to speak to her. When Frank's overbearing aunt dies, the Westons expect him to propose to Emma, but his subsequent actions start a chain of events that shocks the heroine, resulting in the realisation of a truth she previously dismissed. Romola Garai and Jonny Lee Miller star in Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Concluding part. Following the destruction of their home by the McGraths, Tilly and her grandmother are forced to live a life of solitude. Tilly and Katie Drew begin working at Sopwith's mine. Despite the hardships of life in the pit, she enjoys feeling like part of the family. Period drama, starring Carli Norris and Sarah Jane Foster

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A young nun is found dead in a smoke-filled bedroom in what seems like a terrible accident caused by a lit cigarette. But when Richard deduces it must have been murder, there are no shortage of suspects, including other nuns, a priest and the mother superior - as well as an unscrupulous PR man promoting a holy spring in the convent grounds. The question is, with the room locked from the inside, how could anyone have committed the crime in the first place? Kenneth Cranham, Patrick Baladi and Caroline Langrishe guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a missing teenager is found and ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect, a local locksmith

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh finds an unlikely test subject for his revolutionary new artificial heart. Starring Rodger Corser, Nicole da Silva and Ryan Johnson

programme.desc: The Glaswegian detective is drawn into a suicide case where all is not as it seems - despite his usual attempts to stay out of such investigations. It was directed by Gordon Flemyng, who helmed two Doctor Who big screen offerings, and classic episodes of The Avengers, The Saint and Minder

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: After an early frost decimated a big part of their crop, father-and-son team Billy and Billy Junior are excited to sell a fair chunk of their organic fruit and nuts at Birmingham's Moseley Farmers' Market. Meanwhile, farmer Leon is hoping his brother-in-law Peter will pull in the punters to try and then buy some of their home-grown, home-made wines and gins. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on a fairground dodgem, two figurines, a pair of ornate candlesticks, a vintage garden sprayer and a rescued mannequin

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich head to West Sussex, where they come up with ideas for the owners of a narrow garden who want to modernise their space

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise fears the worst after hearing the truth. Gray struggles with Karen's new role, while a distraught Kathy is at a loss over what to do next

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Daniel Sloss also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: Young Adult (2011)
programme.desc: A selfish teenager grows up to be an alcoholic failed writer. When she learns her high-school sweetheart's wife has just had a baby, she refuses to believe he can be happy without her, and returns to her home town to steal him away from his happy family, finding an unlikely ally in a classmate whose adolescence she made a misery. Comedy, starring Charlize Theron, Patrick Wilson and Patton Oswalt

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Ahead of the much-anticipated second series, Mama Ru introduces the 12 contestants competing for the title of the UK's next Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Shivi Ramoutar and Tom Brown, and guest Hugh Dennis, while drinks expert Helen McGinn selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the midweek Premier League fixtures and look ahead to the weekend's contests, including Liverpool v Manchester United

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. At this stage last year, the sole remaining former champion, Shaun Murphy, lost 6-3 to Ali Carter

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to transform a garden for a family in Walsall. With three generations living under the same roof, privacy is paramount, so the designers have their work cut out transforming the plot into a stylish area that the family can relax in, in comfort and away from prying eyes

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Singer Kimberly Wyatt, TV presenter and stylist Darren Kennedy, pioneering DJ Normski and actor Aoife Hinds answer questions on Bob Fosse, The Geisha, electronic music of the 1980s and 1990s and Amy Winehouse

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a celebrity Impressionists edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Debra Stephenson, Luke Kempner, Jan Ravens, Ronni Ancona, Rory Bremner, Jess Robinson, Steve Nallon and Josh Berry

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An exhausted Will faces his last day in ED - but will he be able to make peace with his colleagues in the department? Robyn goes the extra mile to help a conflicted patient, while Rash plucks up the courage to get himself a date

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Bono is being bullied by another boy at school so his grandmother steps in to sort things out in her inimitable style. When her efforts to help only make things even worse for the lad, a mediation session is called for. Cathy's new French boyfriend doesn't make a great first impression, which could be a problem if he is to survive meeting Mrs Brown, and Grandad is back from hospital with a new stairlift - and a prescription for marijuana. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. The first Black Country derby took place today since the Baggies prevailed 5-1 here in the 2011/12 season. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to the divisional play-offs as the remaining eight teams compete for the right to move a step closer to Super Bowl LV

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. The first Black Country derby took place today since the Baggies prevailed 5-1 here in the 2011/12 season. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of vintage film posters

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer and new presenter Brenda Edwards visits an outdoor walking church in east London, while Katie Piper meets a vicar who has become a hit on social media with 60-second sermons

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple hoping to relocate to rural Somerset on a budget of £700,000, looking for a home with enough land for animals and a glamping site. Away from the hunt, Alistair gets stuck in with some Cheddar making in the county where it originated

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles, Marie-Andree and Ajay flee Bangkok for Nepal, where they scour the streets for victims, but tensions arise when Charles loses the group's money in a card game until Marie-Andree overcomes her crisis of conscience to deliver their next prey. In March 1976, Nadine helps Herman and Angela to accumulate evidence by taking undercover photos of the suspects and their private apartment

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester City v Crystal Palace. The Reds won last season's corresponding fixture 2-0 en route to winning the title and another such result would further cement their status as title-favourites. Meanwhile, Spurs and City will have expected victories to boost their own chances of success. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester United, Manchester City v Aston Villa and Reading v Arsenal

programme.title: All the President's Men (1976)
programme.desc: The true story of Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein, who persevered in their investigation of the links between the Watergate break-in and the White House, a scandal that eventually forced the resignation of President Nixon. Alan J Pakula's drama, starring Robert Redford, Dustin Hoffman, Jason Robards and Hal Holbrook

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Gareth and Jodi Todd, three years after they spent a trial week in Australia, to find out where they and their family now call home

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two couples are preparing for Exeter's Farmers' Market. Ex-archaeologist Janet and her husband Andy are hoping to sell some of their chickens to make enough cash to finish Uncle Pat's new home on their farm. Meanwhile, Liv and Henry are selling their vegetables. They have big plans to create an outdoor kitchen on their modest seven-acre farm, and with some exciting news on the horizon, this young couple are praying for all the sales they can get. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a concertina that survived combat in the First World War, a vintage jigsaw, and a cocktail table with a surprising secret compartment. Plus, soft-toy restoration duo Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch are called upon to rescue a much-loved toy dog, made by German manufacturer Steiff, which its owner Anita has treasured ever since it was given to her by her grandparents as a young girl

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Science week comes to a close with physics, focusing on forces, friction and inertia. The Big Read concludes and the Awesome Scientist of the Day is Isaac Newton

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines the remotest part of the solar system, a place that the most mysterious planets - Uranus, Neptune and dwarf planet Pluto - call home. Barely visible to the naked eye, Uranus was once thought to be the furthest planet from the Sun, before Neptune was discovered. Thanks to a rare alignment of the planets in 1976, Voyager 2 was sent for mankind's only flyby of these ice worlds. Even at such cold temperatures, great storms whip around these frozen worlds, which are home to spectacular moons and intricate ring systems. Then in 2015, Nasa's New Horizons probe pushed the frontier even further into space with its extraordinary passage to the dwarf planet Pluto

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: John Higgins v Ronnie O'Sullivan. Coverage of the third quarter-final at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Grand Pier at Weston-super-Mare in Somerset, where experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt pick out some gems to value. Among them are rare medals that once belonged to an early aviator and puppets that look like the Swedish band Abba. Plus, Paul delves into the history of seaside entertainment.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the county's china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwall's highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwall's picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Land's End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Stephen Maguire v Yan Bingtao. The players take to the table at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes for the fourth and concluding quarter-final. Mark Selby and Neil Robertson were seeded to meet at this stage, but Maguire and Bingtao both caused upsets to give themselves a chance of reaching the last four of the prestigious event

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Monty Don and the team look back at the horticultural highlights from 2020 and celebrate the very best inspirational gardens and gardeners up and down the country. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Dolly Parton - Here I Am
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the acclaimed songwriter, which invites viewers to get to know the real Dolly Parton, the woman behind the global phenomenon. The feature-length documentary, directed by Francis Whately, takes the audience on a musical journey through some of her greatest songs, including The Bridge, I Will Always Love You, and 9 To 5. Cameras also capture Dolly celebrating the 50th anniversary of her very first performance at the Grand Ole Opry, and features unprecedented access to the singer herself, as well as collaborators from throughout her career

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. Starbuck returns from the Twelve Colonies and someone aboard Galactica is revealed to be a Cylon. Roslin suspects Baltar of collaboration with the enemy, but fails to keep the presidency out of his hands. Believing the fleet finally free of the Cylon menace, he decrees the immediate colonisation of New Caprica

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Action from the concluding quarter-finals at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the players needed to win six frames to progress

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: A guide to strengthening the immune system during cold and flu season, examining some of the so-called 'miracle' products, superfoods and supplements that claim to help

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates discover a primate rescue centre in Vietnam, explore nomadic areas of Mongolia's capital Ulaan Baatar and pay tribute to ELO in the city of Shanghai

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team plans to build a 'magical library' den for 10-year-old Eleanor, who has cerebral palsy, and struggles to make it to the local library to indulge her passion for books

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: The young cook demonstrates his take on a family favourite - spicy jackfruit curry

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: The young cook is creating a delicious dinner for when dad gets home from work - cosy macaroni. This bowl of smooth, melty gorgeousness has a spicy pumpkin twist

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The presenters are at the zoo this week as Lindsey Russell and Adam Beales meet endangered red pandas and Mwaksy Mudenda visits a precious rhino calf. Plus, viewers' animal-themed posts and videos

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall turns detective to solve CBBC viewers' animal mysteries. From sharks to squid, Steve answers questions about marine animals

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall turns detective to solve CBBC viewers' animal mysteries. From lions to whales, Steve answers questions about the deadliest animals of all

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in rural Somerset that produces one million pots of yoghurt every 24 hours, and meets the herd of cows that produce the milk. Cherry Healey helps out with the UK's biggest blackcurrant harvest and tries out plant-based alternatives to milk, while historian Ruth Goodman reveals the stories behind the electric milk float and the cream tea

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to four more professional dog groomers out to prove their skills. First they tackle a pack of Alaskan malamutes, whose fur well and truly flies when the contestants get to work de-shedding their undercoats. They then give four fluffy puppies their first ever haircut to be revealed in front of their proud owners. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Hill farmer Joyce Campbell prepares spring, and does everything she can to make sure her 800 North Country Cheviot ewes are healthy ahead of birthing season. Meanwhile in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are preparing for the first cattle TB test on their new farm, and in Aberdeenshire, spring has come early for new entrant farmers Isla and George at Little Rowater Farm

programme.title: Food & Drink
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge is joined by Cyrus Todiwala (The Incredible Spice Men) to discuss the benefits of wholesome food, with the host challenging the award-winning Indian chef to come up with an alternative take on a curry to rival his own spice-crusted tuna salad. Comedienne Arabella Weir tries her hardest to fall in love with salad, and drinks expert Joe Wadsack picks a selection of healthy red wines

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist reflects on the remarkable abilities that some plants and animals possess, such as the electric eel that helped inspire the battery and flowers that 'talk' to bees using electrical signals. Early naturalists were puzzled as to where some animals disappeared to each year and the discovery of the swallow's epic migration and the butterfly's magical transformation were scientific stories once cloaked in mystery and controversy

programme.title: The Nun's Story (1959)
programme.desc: An independent-minded woman enters a convent and develops a gift for medicine, while struggling to curb her rebellious nature. She is sent to the Congo to assist a doctor in his work, but suffers a crisis of faith at the outbreak of the Second World War. Drama, based on Kathryn C Hulme's novel, starring Audrey Hepburn, Peter Finch, Peggy Ashcroft, Dean Jagger and Mildred Dunnock

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by experts Michael Baggott and Caroline Hawley on a visit to Haddon Hall in Derbyshire, where the team picks out a selection of interesting antiques and collectibles to be sold at a local auction. Michael finds an impressive scent bottle, and Caroline gets a glimpse into the past by looking through a stereoscopic viewer. Paul also looks around the property, and meets the current owner, Lord Edward Manners

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Further coverage of the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group settle into life in their base in Jaipur, India, as Wayne Sleep and Bobby George have a cut-throat shave, Patti Boulaye and Miriam Margoyles visit a local laundrywoman, and Jan Leeming attends a psychic guru and is astounded at his profound observations on her life. To end the second week, the celebrities take a 150-mile trip to the Taj Mahal, some by train and others by chauffeur-driven car, and the sheer beauty of the world's most impressive monument to love leaves Miriam in tears

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Eventual champion Stuart Bingham was in fine form at this stage last year as he prevailed 6-2 against David Gilbert. Despite not carrying ranking points for the players, this tournament is one of the most prestigious on the calendar and part of the Triple Crown of events, that also includes the World Championship and UK Championship. Ronnie O'Sullivan is the most successful player in the tournament's history, having won on seven occasions, and reached the final a further six times

programme.title: 99 Homes (2014)
programme.desc: A single father desperately trying to support his son and his mother goes to work for the ruthless businessman who evicted them from their home. He is seduced by the glamorous world of high finance and seeks the chance to provide for his family's security - but to get it, he will have to deprive other families of their homes. Drama, starring Andrew Garfield, Michael Shannon and Laura Dern

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of a semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Alexandra Palace in London

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night (1964)
programme.desc: The Beatles, harassed by their manager and Paul's grandfather, embark on a train journey to London for a TV show, but find themselves dogged by all manner of problems, including the inevitable overexcited fans. Musical comedy, starring the Fab Four alongside Wilfrid Brambell, Victor Spinetti and Anna Quayle. Songs include Can't Buy Me Love, Don't Bother Me and the title track

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter D, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, revealing how these entirely different creatures have both adapted to life in the dark. He also looks at gorillas and vampire bats both gained unpleasant reputations within the animal kingdom

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Monty Don and the team look back at the horticultural highlights from 2020 and celebrate the very best inspirational gardens and gardeners up and down the country. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: After the turmoil of 2020 and as a new year gets underway, the team looks at how people can plan to make the countryside a bigger part of their lives? Matt Baker wants to get fitter and tries his hand at cani-cross, Anita Rani straps on her walking boots, Margherita Taylor heads for the woods and Helen Skelton aims to buy local. Tom Heap discovers the joys of 'blue' therapy and Adam Henson gets creative with clay

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British pork, preparing a traditional gala pie and a rolled loin with apricots and ginger. They also explore the role the pig has played in the UK's history and travel to a farm in Gloucestershire to visit a couple raising rare breeds

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session of the final, where the first eight frames are scheduled to take place at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Stuart Bingham won the final two frames of this session against Ali Carter last year in London to take a 5-3 lead into the evening. Jason Mohammad presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are at Normanby Hall in north Lincolnshire, where experts Michael Baggot and Caroline Hawley unearth some favourite antiques among the crowd, including a French poster. Plus, Paul uncovers how the building became a hospital during the First World War

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Highlights of the classic Lauberhorn downhill ski races from Wengen in Switzerland, as the skiers tackled the oldest, fastest and longest downhill on the men's World Cup circuit

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the concluding session of the final at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the first player to reach 10 frames will claim the title. Stuart Bingham became the oldest-ever winner of this tournament at the age of 43 when he defeated Ali Carter 10-8 in last year's showpiece match in London, claiming his most prestigious honour since he won the World Championship in 2015. Carter had fought back from a 5-3 deficit at the end of the first session to go into a 7-5 lead, but Bingham was then able to win four consecutive frames to leave him on the verge of victory. Jason Mohammad presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo

programme.title: Mississippi Grind (2015)
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck gambler finds his fortunes changing when he makes a new friend, who persuades him to compete in a high-stakes poker game in New Orleans. They embark on a road trip alongside the Mississippi river, taking the opportunity to test their skills at every racetrack, casino and pool hall along the way. Drama, starring Ben Mendelsohn, Ryan Reynolds and Sienna Miller

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the showdown against Arthur, the Petrelli brothers find themselves battling each other - and Nathan makes a move that will have global repercussions. Meanwhile, a face-off ensues when Sylar holds Claire, Noah, Meredith and Angela hostage at Primatech. Ando, Matt and Daphne continue their efforts to rescue Hiro - and Suresh may be their only hope. Starring Milo Ventimiglia, Adrian Pasdar and Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Months after the fall of Pinehurst and Primatech, suresh has gone back to driving taxis, while Daphne and Matt are trying to live as an ordinary couple. But Claire's routine is shaken up when she uncovers Nathan's deadly plot to capture those with abilities - and a powerless Hiro is the first victim when he is dragged away by hooded men. Elsewhere, Sylar searches for his real parents. Guest starring Zeljko Ivanek (Damages)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar's mistake has fatal consequences, but she may not be entirely to blame for the tragedy. Kian opens up to Jac about his addiction, but remains unsure that she will stand by him now she knows the truth. Alex and Donna kiss, but Donna still finds herself struggling to put Xavier's memory to rest

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Manchester never sounded better than Herman's Hermits and the Hollies, who both feature in this archive extravaganza

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on June 22, with performances by artists including Magnum, Roxette, Yazz, Big Fun and Sonia, Luciano Pavarotti, Wilson Phillips, MC Hammer and Elton John

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on June 28, with performances by artists including Snap, Jason Donovan, Maureen, Bob Geldof, Bruce Dickinson, Maxi Priest, Elton John and Poison

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Stephen Maguire v Yan Bingtao. The players take to the table at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes for the fourth and concluding quarter-final

programme.title: Dolly Parton: 50 Years at the Opry
programme.desc: Highlights from the singer's performance to celebrate 50 years as a member of the Grand Ole Opry, recorded in Nashville in October 2019. Featuring hits including Jolene, 9 to 5, Coat of Many Colours and I Will Always Love You, as well as appearances by Lady A, Emmylou Harris and Hank Williams Jr

programme.title: Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: The country singer's complete set on the Pyramid stage at Worthy Farm in the now-traditional legends spot on Sunday afternoon, featuring Jolene, Coat of Many Colours, 9 to 5, Islands in the Stream and I Will Always Love You

programme.title: Charley Pride - I'm Just Me
programme.desc: The journey of Charley Pride, from his beginnings as a sharecropper's son on a cotton farm in segregated Mississippi to his career as an American League baseball player and his rise as a trailblazing country music superstar. After arriving in Nashville in 1963, thanks to his boldness and perseverance, Pride managed to parlay a series of fortuitous encounters with music industry insiders into a legacy of hit singles. Contributors include Garth Brooks, Dolly Parton, Brad Paisley, Whoopi Goldberg, Willie Nelson, Darius Rucker and Marty Stuart. Narrated by Tanya Tucker

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Country duo Ben Earle and Crissie Rhodes reveal the songs that shaped their musical journey. They begin with Dolly Parton's iconic track Jolene. Following that comes Patsy Cline, as Crissie recalls singing along to Crazy with her grandmother. Ben then picks country-pop crossover Shania Twain, whose That Don't Impress Me Much certainly made its mark on him. He also plumps for Glen Campbell's legendary Wichita Lineman. The pair also cite critically acclaimed duo the Civil Wars and their spine-tingling live appearance on Later - with Jools Holland

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: As the wildlife cameraman and his family's experience on the Shetland Islands comes to an end, he captures footage of gannets diving for fish, films puffins and their chicks underground, observes a pod of killer whales, and follows an otter family's separation as a year-old cub is pushed away by its mother

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Michael reaches the city of Timbuktu, along with a camel train carrying the giant salt blocks that made the city one of the greatest centres of Islamic learning. Later, Michael finally gets to grips with the heart and soul of the desert

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine has been blackmailed by the traffickers into defending their smuggler, while Ali and Laure follow whatever leads they can tease out of the new line of enquiry, but how much information can they get out of their new arrest? Captain Beckriche and judge Lucie Bordieu must find common ground to prove a link between the international smuggling operation and the murder case. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure and Ali identify 'the Alsatian' and get to work on a tracking device that was hidden under his car by members of a rival gang. Josephine tries to help Souleymane, but her attempts are inadequate. In French

programme.title: Paris
programme.desc: Sandrine Voillet examines how the French capital became the world's first modern city, before discovering the historical inspirations behind fashion designer Christian Lacroix's work. She also meets a rap band during a demonstration and searches for the remains of people who died in the Revolution at the Basilica of Saint Denis

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on June 22, with performances by artists including Magnum, Roxette, Yazz, Big Fun and Sonia, Luciano Pavarotti, Wilson Phillips, MC Hammer and Elton John

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on June 28, with performances by artists including Snap, Jason Donovan, Maureen, Bob Geldof, Bruce Dickinson, Maxi Priest, Elton John and Poison

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Former Scissor Sisters singer Ana Matronic and Martyn Ware, who was in both the Human League and Heaven 17, reveal a playlist packed with disco classics. Each song is handpicked, and as they watch the performances Ana and Martyn reveal the reasons behind their choices. From Donna Summer to the Doctor Who theme tune and the Temptations to Tina Turner, their playlist holds dance-along gems interwoven with candid stories. The remarkable talents of the performers are showcased, as some of the secrets to their success are revealed

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Michael reaches the city of Timbuktu, along with a camel train carrying the giant salt blocks that made the city one of the greatest centres of Islamic learning. Later, Michael finally gets to grips with the heart and soul of the desert

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: As the wildlife cameraman and his family's experience on the Shetland Islands comes to an end, he captures footage of gannets diving for fish, films puffins and their chicks underground, observes a pod of killer whales, and follows an otter family's separation as a year-old cub is pushed away by its mother

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1870s, which was blighted by a dire economic depression. The Potters can no longer rely on breadwinner Graham as he struggles to find work so they join forces with single parent Shazeda to try to survive by making artificial flowers, while there are new arrivals from Ireland in the form of siblings John and Maria Barker, who are horrified by the conditions that would have greeted Irish migrants to Victorian London

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Series following Charley Boorman through 25 countries, as he tries to travel by land and sea from his home in Co Wicklow to Australia on more than 100 local forms of transport. After deciding on a route that avoids the most dangerous places on Earth, he, his producer and his cameraman begin the first leg of the journey across the Irish Sea to England

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott look back at some of the biggest stories of 2020, from the launch of Solar Orbiter to the discovery of phosphine on Venus

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The story of how the solar system will be transformed by the ageing Sun, before coming to an end in approximately eight billion years. Astronomers can predict how it will happen by analysing distant galaxies, stars and planets in their final moments, and the programme brings these predictions to life with a giant scale model of the solar system spread out all over a town in the American Midwest

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: Part two of two. Professor Jim Al-Khalili visits the Gran Sasso National Laboratory in Italy, where scientists are attempting to capture and study dark matter. He also reveals how the discovery that the expansion of the universe is accelerating has led to theories that a mysterious new force called dark energy is in the process of gradually tearing the universe apart

At school in Vietnam, Truc, who's seven, plays lots of games, has a tasty lunch and a little sleep with her friends.
