programme.desc: Peter meets some surprising new friends at Hill Top Farm, but when a big storm hits the woods, it's up to the rabbit to save the day

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in trouble when Glitch's feather collecting mission goes wrong.

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Greengrocer Yusuf finds a little star in a delivery from outer space. The star is very sad, so Yusuf and the people of Biggleton do their best to cheer it up. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Robert tells Justin his Giggle Gasket has broken, meaning he cannot laugh, so Justin does everything he can to help his best friend get his laughter back

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Andy inadvertently loses some kapok tree seeds that his safari park boss Mr Hammond had been hoping to plant in the chimp enclosure, he jets off to Africa in his safari-mobile to track down some more. Shrinking down to the size of a baby chimpanzees, Andy embarks on an epic adventure with chimps, swinging through the trees in search of replacement kapok seeds

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk along the beach to go paddling in the sea.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's Mum, Jess has just married Doug's Dad, Simon. The new family get home to discover a lost pup hiding in the wedding presents. Evie wants to keep him and call him Waffle

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: When the pair's friend Tony comes to play, their pet rabbit Wiggles scratches him on the hand

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat: Rockpool Tales
programme.desc: Old Jack is using his telescope when he spots Ernie building a sandcastle, and remembers when he used to make them on the beach when he was a boy with Little Ernie and Little Sailor Sue. Elsewhere, Sally the Starfish wants to go on an adventure in the Big Sandy World with Andrew the Anemone

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley helps to harvest mistletoe, JB Gill visits a tractor factory and Rory Crawford finds out about hedgehogs. Storm also shares a snowy poem and reveals how soup is made

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Ghana, five-year-old Tyra learns English at school, and plays games in the playground. In Russia, Liza, who is seven, spends the morning at the playground trying to beat her twin brother, Styopa, at standing on one leg. in Italy, seven-year-old Mario sings a song in front of a whole choir

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends learn that sharing can be either fun or not such a good idea

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Brothers Bobby and Lenny are busy making robot costumes out of cardboard boxes

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: ory visits Eva and her pet cat Chewie, who is going to the vet to check his weight. Afterwards they take him home to try new ways to help him lose weight. Ferne joins a vet at a wildlife hospital and finds out how they care for local wildlife

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day learns about baby animals that are great at teamwork, including young meerkats in Africa, and Emperor penguins in the Antarctic

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Lb create Sploshy Dropper to help the poptato seeds grow

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble goes to Chef Tumble's restaurant where everything is either too big or too small, while Justin and his friends visit a model village

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns why things float and discovers how sand on a beach is made.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster finds it difficult when everyone has to do things the opposite way around.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter's plan to collect the year's first bluebell for his mother takes an unexpected turn when he finds it on Mr Tod's doorstep

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo is having a sleepover at Gran Gran's house and wonders why the moon is a funny shape. Gran Gran explains to JoJo why the moon appears to change shape in the night sky, and together they make a moon diary for JoJo to take home to Mummy and Daddy.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle feels left out of the Brooklyn-Bells' fun, so he sets off to find his doggy family

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan visits Aaron and his pet ducks, and together they clean out their home and fill up the duck pond

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace looks back at some of her favourite machines that are electric, fast and useful

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with socks and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Click Clack becomes a trainee kite pilot and learns how to navigate a kite

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: When Kid spoils Harriet and Osbourne's carefully laid treasure trail, Timmy turns detective and follows a set of clues to claim the prize

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry, Kari and Thingy try to decide what to play

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: African folk tale, explaining why the eagle rules the skies

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns about clouds and rain and discovers how a radiator is made

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Gran's signature Saint Lucian banana bread, they're excited to bake a batch of it! But there's one problem - cake mix has been spilt on the recipe, so they don't know how long to bake it for!

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: May storms bring lashing wind and rain to the island, threatening the birthday plans of Katie Morag and her grandmothers

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Inspired by a trip to the art gallery, Sarah and Duck give Moon painting lessons

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Marv and Charlie try to persuade Lola that playing alone on the see-saw is no fun

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wants to help Mo out with chores around the house

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Baby Soup Dragon is upset because his mother will not let him use her big ladle to help make the soup, so Tiny and Small tell him he can give them a hand instead

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Tombliboo Ooo makes himself ill by drinking too much Pinky Ponk juice, so his friends play music to make him feel better

programme.title: Tee and Mo Song Time
programme.desc: At the end of a busy day, Tee is ready for his bed, but refuses to admit it. As the sun goes down, Mo gets Tee ready for his bedtime, and by the time the stars are shining in the sky, Tee is tucked up and ready for a good night's sleep

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Another chance to see astronaut Tim Peake reading Goodnight Spaceman, by Michelle Robinson, from on board the International Space Station

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a corner plot in Wallasey, Merseyside, a mid-terrace house in Hyde in Manchester and a bungalow in Great Barr, Birmingham

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Host Eric Knowles enters the world of travel and transport, as he takes a look back at some of the most interesting past purchases in that genre. He also meets a group of students and their treasured vintage fire engine, dubbed Jezebel, and offers viewers advice on how to spot a bargain

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook dishes for when the temperature drops and the weather calls for sugar and spice and good hearty cooking. This means oxtail soup with herby dumplings and a new take on an old favourite, treacle sponge

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Andrew Treagust needed a change of pace and thought this lay in New Zealand, where he could follow his dream of becoming a snowboard instructor. Partner Emma was less than convinced, having a job she loved in the UK and two young boys with close ties to their grandparents. Six years after the family sampled a week 'down under', the programme catches up with them to see how their lives have changed. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: After an early frost decimated a big part of their crop, father-and-son team Billy and Billy Junior are excited to sell a fair chunk of their organic fruit and nuts at Birmingham's Moseley Farmers' Market. Meanwhile, farmer Leon is hoping his brother-in-law Peter will pull in the punters to try and then buy some of their home-grown, home-made wines and gins. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on a fairground dodgem, two figurines, a pair of ornate candlesticks, a vintage garden sprayer and a rescued mannequin

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard is challenged to cook a calorific banquet for the Dragon Pro Wrestlers in Newport. These professional athletes pack a whopping punch when it comes to their diet eating up to 4,000 calories when preparing for a bout. It remains to be seen therefore whether Matt's vegan meal can provide them with the energy needed to get them though to an important, upcoming wrestling event

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise fears the worst after hearing the truth. Gray struggles with Karen's new role, while a distraught Kathy is at a loss over what to do next

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Daniel Sloss also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: Young Adult (2011)
programme.desc: A selfish teenager grows up to be an alcoholic failed writer. When she learns her high-school sweetheart's wife has just had a baby, she refuses to believe he can be happy without her, and returns to her home town to steal him away from his happy family, finding an unlikely ally in a classmate whose adolescence she made a misery. Comedy, starring Charlize Theron, Patrick Wilson and Patton Oswalt

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Ahead of the much-anticipated second series, Mama Ru introduces the 12 contestants competing for the title of the UK's next Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Shivi Ramoutar and Tom Brown, and guest Hugh Dennis, while drinks expert Helen McGinn selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the midweek Premier League fixtures and look ahead to the weekend's contests, including Liverpool v Manchester United

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. At this stage last year, the sole remaining former champion, Shaun Murphy, lost 6-3 to Ali Carter

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to transform a garden for a family in Walsall. With three generations living under the same roof, privacy is paramount, so the designers have their work cut out transforming the plot into a stylish area that the family can relax in, in comfort and away from prying eyes

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Singer Kimberly Wyatt, TV presenter and stylist Darren Kennedy, pioneering DJ Normski and actor Aoife Hinds answer questions on Bob Fosse, The Geisha, electronic music of the 1980s and 1990s and Amy Winehouse

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a celebrity Impressionists edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Debra Stephenson, Luke Kempner, Jan Ravens, Ronni Ancona, Rory Bremner, Jess Robinson, Steve Nallon and Josh Berry

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An exhausted Will faces his last day in ED - but will he be able to make peace with his colleagues in the department? Robyn goes the extra mile to help a conflicted patient, while Rash plucks up the courage to get himself a date

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Bono is being bullied by another boy at school so his grandmother steps in to sort things out in her inimitable style. When her efforts to help only make things even worse for the lad, a mediation session is called for. Cathy's new French boyfriend doesn't make a great first impression, which could be a problem if he is to survive meeting Mrs Brown, and Grandad is back from hospital with a new stairlift - and a prescription for marijuana. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. The first Black Country derby took place today since the Baggies prevailed 5-1 here in the 2011/12 season. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to the divisional play-offs as the remaining eight teams compete for the right to move a step closer to Super Bowl LV

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. The first Black Country derby took place today since the Baggies prevailed 5-1 here in the 2011/12 season. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of vintage film posters

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer and new presenter Brenda Edwards visits an outdoor walking church in east London, while Katie Piper meets a vicar who has become a hit on social media with 60-second sermons

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple hoping to relocate to rural Somerset on a budget of £700,000, looking for a home with enough land for animals and a glamping site. Away from the hunt, Alistair gets stuck in with some Cheddar making in the county where it originated

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: Carol Vorderman: Closer to Home
programme.desc: Carol revisits her schooldays in Rhyl and meets some of the region's inspirational teachers, including a woman who has been teaching children karate since the age of 12. She also has a go at harness-racing, joins a school science lesson involving a zip line, and is reunited with her step-family for a birthday celebration

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen takes a sideways look at Alternative Wales, featuring clips of belly dancers, hamster lovers and pylon enthusiast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles, Marie-Andree and Ajay flee Bangkok for Nepal, where they scour the streets for victims, but tensions arise when Charles loses the group's money in a card game until Marie-Andree overcomes her crisis of conscience to deliver their next prey. In March 1976, Nadine helps Herman and Angela to accumulate evidence by taking undercover photos of the suspects and their private apartment

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester City v Crystal Palace. The Reds won last season's corresponding fixture 2-0 en route to winning the title and another such result would further cement their status as title-favourites. Meanwhile, Spurs and City will have expected victories to boost their own chances of success. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester United, Manchester City v Aston Villa and Reading v Arsenal

programme.title: All the President's Men (1976)
programme.desc: The true story of Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein, who persevered in their investigation of the links between the Watergate break-in and the White House, a scandal that eventually forced the resignation of President Nixon. Alan J Pakula's drama, starring Robert Redford, Dustin Hoffman, Jason Robards and Hal Holbrook

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's briefing with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic

programme.desc: Margherita Taylor heads to Rutland, England's smallest county, to help a young mother to carve out a new country life for her family with a budget of £450,000. Margherita also visits Rutland Water, where she gets a hands-on course in fly-fishing to find out what she might catch for dinner

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lenny makes a final attempt to survive when faced with a vengeful Amber, as time runs out on Alex and Rory's search for their father. Elsewhere, Eve struggles to come to terms that Cameron could be capable of being Poppy's stalker and what she should do next

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: Andrew Kerr and guests look at the week's events at Westminster and Holyrood

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Rev Jane Denniston of Campsie Parish Church and Fr John Deighan of St Machan's in Lennoxtown present from the foyer of BBC Glasgow, with live music by Rachel Hair and Ron Jappy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich head to West Sussex, where they come up with ideas for the owners of a narrow garden who want to modernise their space

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: A Friday evening trip out in the car ends in a fatal crash, but the driver refuses to accept his driving is to blame. The collision team must find the evidence to prove the real story of the night

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer and new presenter Brenda Edwards visits an outdoor walking church in east London, while Katie Piper meets a vicar who has become a hit on social media with 60-second sermons

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple hoping to relocate to rural Somerset on a budget of £700,000, looking for a home with enough land for animals and a glamping site. Away from the hunt, Alistair gets stuck in with some Cheddar making in the county where it originated

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges have scoured the country, visiting 27 incredible homes and now, the finalists gather as the judges choose the overall winner

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles, Marie-Andree and Ajay flee Bangkok for Nepal, where they scour the streets for victims, but tensions arise when Charles loses the group's money in a card game until Marie-Andree overcomes her crisis of conscience to deliver their next prey. In March 1976, Nadine helps Herman and Angela to accumulate evidence by taking undercover photos of the suspects and their private apartment

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester City v Crystal Palace. The Reds won last season's corresponding fixture 2-0 en route to winning the title and another such result would further cement their status as title-favourites. Meanwhile, Spurs and City will have expected victories to boost their own chances of success. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Motherwell v Rangers. Steven Thompson presents action from the Premiership clash at Fir Park. Plus, a round-up of Saturday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester United, Manchester City v Aston Villa and Reading v Arsenal

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a corner plot in Wallasey, Merseyside, a mid-terrace house in Hyde in Manchester and a bungalow in Great Barr, Birmingham

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Andrew Treagust needed a change of pace and thought this lay in New Zealand, where he could follow his dream of becoming a snowboard instructor. Partner Emma was less than convinced, having a job she loved in the UK and two young boys with close ties to their grandparents. Six years after the family sampled a week 'down under', the programme catches up with them to see how their lives have changed. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Host Eric Knowles enters the world of travel and transport, as he takes a look back at some of the most interesting past purchases in that genre. He also meets a group of students and their treasured vintage fire engine, dubbed Jezebel, and offers viewers advice on how to spot a bargain

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook dishes for when the temperature drops and the weather calls for sugar and spice and good hearty cooking. This means oxtail soup with herby dumplings and a new take on an old favourite, treacle sponge

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor heads to Rutland, England's smallest county, to help a young mother to carve out a new country life for her family with a budget of £450,000. Margherita also visits Rutland Water, where she gets a hands-on course in fly-fishing to find out what she might catch for dinner

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: After an early frost decimated a big part of their crop, father-and-son team Billy and Billy Junior are excited to sell a fair chunk of their organic fruit and nuts at Birmingham's Moseley Farmers' Market. Meanwhile, farmer Leon is hoping his brother-in-law Peter will pull in the punters to try and then buy some of their home-grown, home-made wines and gins. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on a fairground dodgem, two figurines, a pair of ornate candlesticks, a vintage garden sprayer and a rescued mannequin

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich head to West Sussex, where they come up with ideas for the owners of a narrow garden who want to modernise their space

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise fears the worst after hearing the truth. Gray struggles with Karen's new role, while a distraught Kathy is at a loss over what to do next

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Give My Head Peace
programme.desc: Da and Ma are both delighted to discover that they are not legally married, while Uncle Andy and Begbie make a Tik Tok video. Award-winning comedy, starring Tim McGarry and Paddy Jenkins

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Daniel Sloss also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: Young Adult (2011)
programme.desc: A selfish teenager grows up to be an alcoholic failed writer. When she learns her high-school sweetheart's wife has just had a baby, she refuses to believe he can be happy without her, and returns to her home town to steal him away from his happy family, finding an unlikely ally in a classmate whose adolescence she made a misery. Comedy, starring Charlize Theron, Patrick Wilson and Patton Oswalt

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Ahead of the much-anticipated second series, Mama Ru introduces the 12 contestants competing for the title of the UK's next Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Thailand's Youth Rebellion
programme.desc: For five months young protesters in Thailand have been challenging a government backed by the formidable power of the armed forces and the monarchy. For the first time a mass movement is openly calling for reform of the monarchy, shattering a taboo surrounding the royal institution's near-sacred status. Influenced by last year's protests in Hong Kong, young Thais have used social media and drawn on popular culture to outwit the efforts of the Thai police to shut their movement down. An unpopular government has so far been reluctant to use harsh measures against them. The monarchy has lost much of the reverence it enjoyed under the long reign of King Bhumibol, who died four years ago. His less popular son, King Vajiralongkorn, has alarmed many Thais by accumulating even more power and living overseas. But he is now back in Thailand, meeting Thai royalists and encouraging them to challenge the student-led protests with demonstrations of loyalty. The stage is set for a dangerous confronta

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: As President-elect Joe Biden takes the oath of office to become the 46th president of the United States, what will his priorities be? programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: In September last year hundreds of campers were celebrating the Labour Day holiday at Mammoth Pools in California's Sierra National Forest. They had come for a leisurely bank holiday weekend, but within hours they found themselves surrounded by California's biggest ever single wildfire. As the flames advanced towards them, it seemed they had no way out. Using footage shot by the campers themselves, and with compelling interviews with survivors and the emergency services, Our World tells the extraordinary story of the holidaymakers who found themselves trapped by an almighty wildfire

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: For the birds and animals of the Highlands, the summer offers a chance to raise offspring before the days shorten and the storms of autumn arrive. Seabirds flock to the island of Handa off the Sutherland Coast, where 30,000 pairs of guillemots breed annually. Handa is also the location for a spectacular wild drama ending with the tiny chicks, known as jumplings, leaping for their lives from the 400-ft cliffs. Cameras also reveal life in the great rolling expanse of wetland known as the Flow Country, whose blanket peat bog stores more carbon than all of the forests in the entire UK

programme.desc: The little rabbit shops for a very special present for Sula, but decides he would rather keep it for himself

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The little rabbit tries hula hooping, but finds it very difficult

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Fung asks that Po return a favour - a task that leaves the panda in a spot of bother

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia is chased when she’s out, and she comes across something that she is not expecting

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Coinneach Clogaid creates a trap for the other competitors today but, fortunately, Crùbag is able to sweep Coinneach out of the way

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Lilly from Lochaber talks about her favourite hobby, skiing

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: The danger agents are at Squawk's super-secret Wild West testing facility, home to a society of Penbots, robots who have a rather familiar face

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Seachd Là (News Review)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Mairi MacInnes sings Sheas a Mhàthair and Niall MacLennan and Alasdair MacPhee perform Psalm 42. Plus, Derek Murray shares the role faith plays in his life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: The whole country is in lockdown and Sweeny is working out how to keep the croft going during these challenging times. With no camera crew permitted to visit for now, he is also getting to grips with self-shooting

programme.title: Na Tiristich (From Tiree with Love)
programme.desc: Life on Tiree, the most westerly island in the Inner Hebrides

programme.title: Ceol is Cradh
programme.desc: Mischa MacPherson looks at mental health struggles in the music industry, with Corrina Hewat and Ross Ainslie offering a powerful and personal insight into this issue

programme.title: Niteworks: Obair Oidhche
programme.desc: Vicky Kilgour introduces a a showcase concert for the talented band, from Glasgow University. The support artists are the Elephant Sessions and INYAL

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Frank lacks one last piece of evidence to make everything fall into place, and Erik does what he can to prevent him from grasping it. Ingrid settles things with both Alfred and Erik and dares to look ahead for the first time, but Erik has to make a choice. Should he continue to lie or reveal who he really is? In Norwegian

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: Highlights from the 2018 music festival, featuring Amy Macdonald, Brass Gumbo, and Fun Lovin' Criminals

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: The dealers den pitches up in Newport, Gwent, where Aidan Pass has double trouble with a pair of petrol-heads, the Duke falls for a beautiful watercolour, and every dealers' favourite - gold - makes mega bucks

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as Kelly-Anne and Craig from Greenock, Kent couple Paul and Catharine, and husband-and-wife team Daniel and Hind from London use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leave with nothing

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants - Clare-Louise, Jennifer, Steve and James - pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Harriet has made things very bad for Dawn, Paul gambles again, and Vinny makes a shocking discovery

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Faye plucks up the courage and tells Tim about how Ray tried to rape her, Steve finds Peter unconscious in the ginnel, and Asha reveals she's split from Corey

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: In the summer of 1967, Morse and Thursday are both devastated by Joan's abrupt departure from Oxford. The young detective responds to his loss by throwing himself into his work, investigating the suspicious drowning of a scientist who was part of a research team working on an early computer. At first it appears to be a simple suicide - until two more bodies are found in the river

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Happy Birthday Mr Bean
programme.desc: How the sitcom character - who featured in just 14 original episodes - has gone on to become a global superstar with an animated series and two top-grossing feature films. Rowan Atkinson's alter-ego has become an online phenomenon, with more than 110 million online fans and growing. This documentary features classic clips and interviews with Richard Curtis, Tony Robinson and Angus Deayton

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole juggles the realities of his job and his duty to be a good parent, taking his daughter to work as he and Murtaugh unite to foil a gang running a lottery scam. Away from the case, Roger receives a surprise visit from his father-in-law and it is not long before the pair clash. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: As Fizz gets ready to travel to another dimension populated entirely by other Fizzes, Magoo turns up and thinks that Weasel has a new kitten.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for billionaire Bruce Payne, aka the superhero, Ratman. When Ratman is kidnapped, Magoo decides to save the day.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Having completed Project Sentinel, The Mechanic sets his sights on destroying The Hood. While International Rescue scramble to save their former foe, Brains goes on a dangerous mission to stop The Mechanic once and for all.

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: There are yucky surprises and, up against his challengers, there's an egg and spoon race. Who will win and who faces a messy forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean climbs into his favourite tree to stop it from being chopped down.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket is selling up and moving on... but what will happen to Mr. Bean?

programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis talks herself into throwing a fake funeral for Uncle Gene

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the kids are waylaid by a tricky monkey on the way to the Forbidden City.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by comedian and host of Winning Combination Omid Djalili, and chefs Pip Lacey and James Tanner

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a colourful menu to banish winter blues. On the menu are pea, pancetta and chicken tray bake, baked eggs served with roasted tomato and potato bread, sea bass with preserved lemon potatoes, and a peach upside-down cake

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin and Stephen Terry continue heading south through Wales en route to Pembrokeshire, and upon reaching the coast, James cooks up his Marloes Sands seafood parcel. Stephen meets young chocolatier Liam Burgess, who helps him make a ry special one-off chocolate bar for his travelling companion, and James takes to the water in a canoe to search for rock samphire with a local forager

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of Grenada, where he discovers why the spice is so important to the island. He prepares pork with sweet potato mash and nutmeg cream sauce, and makes a martini using a local chocolate liquor

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which three pairs of contestants use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. Looking for the winning words today are married couple George and Sue, mother and daughter Shirley and Laura, and Manchester flatmates Rob and Adam

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Emily Atack, Brenda Edwards and Martin Kemp all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Blob, Grandfather Clock, Bush Baby, Viking and Harlequin take to the stage once more, as judges Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Mo Gilligan try to guess the mystery celebrities singing inside the elaborate costumes. Last week, Jonathan correctly guessed that Swan was former EastEnders star Martine McCutcheon. Can he make it two in a row? Presented by Joel Dommett

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the spinning-chair singing contest, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The four coaches will once again be selecting their teams through blind auditions, where looks play no part in the selection process and each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Chizzy Akudolu, Shirley Ballas, Roman Kemp and Iain Stirling to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: The Nutty Professor (1996)
programme.desc: An obese, terminally shy professor invents a potion that transforms him into a slim, loud-mouthed charmer. His new streamlined self immediately sets out to woo the woman of his dreams - but soon starts to ride roughshod over everything he used to care about. Remake of the 1963 Jerry Lewis comedy, starring Eddie Murphy, Jada Pinkett Smith, James Coburn and Larry Miller

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tends to Fizz's toothache, but Magoo turns up, thinking he's busting into an animal testing lab.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds take the camper van on holiday, and end up at the bottom of the sea. Dave has to resort to a resourceful solution to get help.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The GDF begin testing experimental rescue machines

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: A mega hopscotch course and a unique clothes competition are just two of the challenges facing Dare Master Danny and his competitors!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean takes on the Bruisers at the local skateboard park, using only a mobility scooter.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket becomes inconsolable when Scrapper goes missing. Mr. Bean investigates.

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis uses a cursed code to accidentally unleash a video game on the hotel

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Ready to emerge from her father's shadow, Zee Zatara seeks fame and acclaim performing for audiences at the pier.

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Frankie, Vick and Sam challenge each other to answer questions beginning with the word How. Sam shows the team how to tuck into a tasty worm treat, Frankie explains how a mistake rewrote a famous fairy tale, and Vick shows Frankie how balloons go bang with nothing more than a cotton wool ball.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Teen Titans Go takes a comedic look at the superheroes' lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens.

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter walks through Cornwall and Devon, which boast more than 5,000 miles of footpaths, from the rugged rocks of Dartmoor to the picturesque port of Padstow. In the second episode, Julia walks five miles of the stunning north Cornish coast, from popular picturesque Padstow to the surfing hotspot of Trevone Bay. Along the way she experiences dramatic coastal landscapes featured in Poldark, before getting up close to a herd of Fallow Deer in the historic Prideaux Estate

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes ITN's Tom Bradby and Dragons' Den's star Deborah Meaden to Hampshire to enjoy brunch cocktails and celebrate champion racehorses

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a colourful menu to banish winter blues. On the menu are pea, pancetta and chicken tray bake, baked eggs served with roasted tomato and potato bread, sea bass with preserved lemon potatoes, and a peach upside-down cake

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a new garden for an eight-year-old girl in Nottingham, despite the worst weather they have ever experienced. Tilly Sawford was severely scalded in a freak accident six years ago and continues to require treatment to aid her recovery, leading to the staff at her local hospital recommending the family have a safe garden created for them, with a bold colour scheme and surprise areas for all of the children

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A birthday cake sets a house on fire, a toddler puts an iPad in a washing machine and a cow breaks into a home to use the swimming pool

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Suzi Perry, Mark Porter, Rosemarie Ford and Louis Walsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome a dozen new celebrities who compete to become champions of the rink. This year's contestants - six of whom will perform tonight - are Billie Shepherd, Colin Jackson, Denise Van Outen, Faye Brookes, Graham Bell, Jason Donovan, Joe-Warren Plant, Lady Leshurr, Myleene Klass, Rebekah Vardy, Rufus Hound and Sonny Jay. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Six more contestants have 15 questions, four lifelines and one chance to win £1million as the quiz hosted by Jeremy Clarkson returns. Donald Fear became the series' sixth jackpot winner in 2020, but it remains to be seen whether any of the latest players have what it takes to become the seventh millionaire

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: For Alice, Harry and their daughter, Charlotte, moving into their new home should have been a dream come true. But it turns into a nightmare when Alice discovers Harry dead at the bottom of the stairs in the property he designed. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son hit the road together again, but this time they are staying closer to home and heading for Europe as they travel from Essex to Italy's Amalfi coast. Their first stop is the Netherlands, where they try out the sport of canal jumping, before the pair don Lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion, and take on the world's tallest freestanding climbing wall

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders: Catching the Gameshow Killer
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama, this documentary tells the real story behind the Pembrokeshire Murders. After murdering four people in Pembrokeshire, John Cooper eluded prosecution for 20 years until a new detective team and advances in forensic science finally saw him put behind bars in 2011. For the first time, the key people involved in the case reveal their role in bringing the serial killer to justice

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which three pairs of contestants use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the spinning-chair singing contest, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The four coaches will once again be selecting their teams through blind auditions, where looks play no part in the selection process and each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Chizzy Akudolu, Shirley Ballas, Roman Kemp and Iain Stirling to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: The Nutty Professor (1996)
programme.desc: An obese, terminally shy professor invents a potion that transforms him into a slim, loud-mouthed charmer. His new streamlined self immediately sets out to woo the woman of his dreams - but soon starts to ride roughshod over everything he used to care about. Remake of the 1963 Jerry Lewis comedy, starring Eddie Murphy, Jada Pinkett Smith, James Coburn and Larry Miller

programme.desc: Mavis uses a cursed code to accidentally unleash a video game on the hotel

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Ready to emerge from her father's shadow, Zee Zatara seeks fame and acclaim performing for audiences at the pier.

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Frankie, Vick and Sam challenge each other to answer questions beginning with the word How. Sam shows the team how to tuck into a tasty worm treat, Frankie explains how a mistake rewrote a famous fairy tale, and Vick shows Frankie how balloons go bang with nothing more than a cotton wool ball.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Teen Titans Go takes a comedic look at the superheroes' lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens.

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter walks through Cornwall and Devon, which boast more than 5,000 miles of footpaths, from the rugged rocks of Dartmoor to the picturesque port of Padstow. In the second episode, Julia walks five miles of the stunning north Cornish coast, from popular picturesque Padstow to the surfing hotspot of Trevone Bay. Along the way she experiences dramatic coastal landscapes featured in Poldark, before getting up close to a herd of Fallow Deer in the historic Prideaux Estate

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes ITN's Tom Bradby and Dragons' Den's star Deborah Meaden to Hampshire to enjoy brunch cocktails and celebrate champion racehorses

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a colourful menu to banish winter blues. On the menu are pea, pancetta and chicken tray bake, baked eggs served with roasted tomato and potato bread, sea bass with preserved lemon potatoes, and a peach upside-down cake

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a new garden for an eight-year-old girl in Nottingham, despite the worst weather they have ever experienced. Tilly Sawford was severely scalded in a freak accident six years ago and continues to require treatment to aid her recovery, leading to the staff at her local hospital recommending the family have a safe garden created for them, with a bold colour scheme and surprise areas for all of the children

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A birthday cake sets a house on fire, a toddler puts an iPad in a washing machine and a cow breaks into a home to use the swimming pool

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Suzi Perry, Mark Porter, Rosemarie Ford and Louis Walsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome a dozen new celebrities who compete to become champions of the rink. This year's contestants - six of whom will perform tonight - are Billie Shepherd, Colin Jackson, Denise Van Outen, Faye Brookes, Graham Bell, Jason Donovan, Joe-Warren Plant, Lady Leshurr, Myleene Klass, Rebekah Vardy, Rufus Hound and Sonny Jay. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Six more contestants have 15 questions, four lifelines and one chance to win £1million as the quiz hosted by Jeremy Clarkson returns. Donald Fear became the series' sixth jackpot winner in 2020, but it remains to be seen whether any of the latest players have what it takes to become the seventh millionaire

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: For Alice, Harry and their daughter, Charlotte, moving into their new home should have been a dream come true. But it turns into a nightmare when Alice discovers Harry dead at the bottom of the stairs in the property he designed. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son hit the road together again, but this time they are staying closer to home and heading for Europe as they travel from Essex to Italy's Amalfi coast. Their first stop is the Netherlands, where they try out the sport of canal jumping, before the pair don Lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion, and take on the world's tallest freestanding climbing wall

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders: Catching the Gameshow Killer
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama, this documentary tells the real story behind the Pembrokeshire Murders. After murdering four people in Pembrokeshire, John Cooper eluded prosecution for 20 years until a new detective team and advances in forensic science finally saw him put behind bars in 2011. For the first time, the key people involved in the case reveal their role in bringing the serial killer to justice

However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspicious death in an army barracks, aided by Joe and an enthusiastic member of the military police. Sensing a connection to Ollie, a soldier killed in Afghanistan, Vera interviews the victim's loyal comrades and Ollie's grieving family, discovering a traumatic event that nobody wants to talk about. programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary offering a behind the scenes look at life inside the Ritz Hotel in London, one of the most famous, traditional luxury hotels in the world. The programmes follows some of the famous faces that visit the hotels, including Richard E Grant, Anna Wintour and Mo Salah, and the work of hotel staff as they prepare for their famous afternoon tea. With consumers spending less and ever more luxury hotels opening their doors, how does one of the most well-known hotels in the world stand out from the rest?

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails, and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of 29-year-old Niall is made difficult by those closest to him, who seem intent on obscuring the facts, and it is not until the body of a drug dealer washes up on a beach that the dreadful truth begins to emerge. While coping with revelations of her own, Vera sifts through evidence of the grief, love and addiction that characterised Niall's life as she tries to find out why someone wanted him dead. With Judy Parfitt (Call the Midwife) and Phyllis Logan (Downton Abbey)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
