programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks along the Isle of Man's stunning coast and visits Sprotbrough in South Yorkshire

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador whose cancer is eating away its front leg, by replacing its tumour with a custom-made implant, and uses the same technique to save the knee of a border collie. Senior surgeon Michael Hamilton also tends a chinchilla that needs urgent treatment on its arm bones after falling from a shelf

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Soho and Milllwall, with fascinating facts for locals and tourists alike. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Meet the people who have followed their own call of the wild to establish relationships with city animals, and the creatures that have adapted to thrive in this urban jungle

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part

programme.title: Dentist on the Job (1961)
programme.desc: Two newly qualified dentists unwittingly get involved in the invention and promotion of a new toothpaste. Comedy, starring Bob Monkhouse, Ronnie Stevens, Kenneth Connor, Shirley Eaton, Eric Barker, Richard Wattis and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: High Treason (1951)
programme.desc: Officers of MI5 and Special branch join forces to deal with a wave of sabotage attacks that have been sweeping the nation. Spy thriller, starring Liam Redmond and Andre Morell

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A distressed teenager explores cures for hair-loss, and a professional singer tries to save her career by learning to control her severe acid reflux. A mother fears her six-year-old son will be a target for bullying because of his psoriasis, and doctors help an obese father reverse damage done to his liver

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom and Roy take up Sally's offer of a week in a Highland croft, where she was going to stay with her boyfriend Mark before his death. While Roy quickly adapts to country life, Tom is soon bored and invites a recently dumped Amber to join them. However, a farcical situation ensues with the arrival of a couple of unexpected visitors

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: The Last Day of WW1
programme.desc: An account of how soldiers continued to be killed in battle hours after the armistice between the Allies and Germany had been signed in 1918. Presented by Michael Palin

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Jamie infiltrates a band of anti-capitalist terrorists who are using a state-of-the-art sniper's rifle, but his MI5 contact in the group loses her nerve and reveals his identity during preparations for their next hit. As Box 500 races to crack a coded message giving his location, Henno decides to take matters into his own hands and Red Troop embarks on a tense rescue bid. Drama, starring Jamie Draven and Ross Kemp

programme.title: Blue Iguana (2018)
programme.desc: Eddie and Paul, former prisoners working in a New York diner, are made an offer they cannot refuse by English lawyer Katherine Rookwood. Romantic comedy, starring Sam Rockwell, Phoebe Fox and Ben Schwartz

programme.title: What's Cooking? (2000)
programme.desc: The members of four diverse families meet up in their respective homes to celebrate Thanksgiving, where the growing Americanisation of the younger generation becomes ever more apparent to their parents, and tangled emotional battles are played out. Drama exploring multiculturalism in Los Angeles, starring Dennis Haysbert, Joan Chen, Julianna Margulies and Kyra Sedgewick

programme.title: Centrespread (1981)
programme.desc: A photographer in a dystopian future searches for a nude model he can turn into a star, a quest that leads him to question the society he has become a part of. Sci-fi drama, starring Kylie Foster and Paul Trahair

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Last Day of WW1
programme.desc: An account of how soldiers continued to be killed in battle hours after the armistice between the Allies and Germany had been signed in 1918. Presented by Michael Palin

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series presented by Andrew White, featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere

programme.title: The Secret Garden
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. The story of a spoiled little English girl who is orphaned in India and sent to live with her uncle where she discovers a garden

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of The Three Little Pigs read by Rik Mayall

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Tony Palmer's landmark series examines the effect the death of Beatles manager Brian Epstein had on the Swinging Sixties. Featuring archive footage of the Rolling Stones, Jimi Hendrix and the Who

programme.title: All At Sea (1970)
programme.desc: A boy travelling on a ship intends to deliver a package to his pen pal in Tangiers, but the parcel goes missing after being falsely identified as contraband. Family drama, starring Gary Smith and Stephen Mallett

programme.title: The Fast Lady (1963)
programme.desc: A cycling enthusiast on a modest civil servant's salary gives up his beloved bike to a buy a luxury car to impress a local magistrate's daughter. However, he first has to pass his driving test to stand any chance of winning her heart - and in the process ends up in a wild car-chase after a gang of robbers. Comedy, with Julie Christie, Stanley Baxter, James Robertson Justice, Leslie Phillips and Dick Emery

programme.title: I'm a Stranger (1952)
programme.desc: An Englishman comes to India to claim his inheritance, only to find his grandfather's will has gone missing. To discover what has become of it and acquire his rightful fortune, he needs the help of a detective, a window cleaner - and movie star Greta Gynt. Comedy, starring Patric Doonan and James Hayter, with Gynt playing herself

programme.title: Lunch Hour (1961)
programme.desc: A junior executive turns his mind to romance, and sets off for a night of passion after reserving a room at a hotel - only for his torrid plans to go awry when his short-term relationship with a designer takes an unexpected turn. Comedy, starring Robert Stephens, Shirley Anne Field, Michael Robbins and Nigel Davenport

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: Strictly Confidential (1959)
programme.desc: Two conmen fresh out of prison are given prestigious jobs by a widow - not realising she has a devious scheme in mind. Comedy, starring Richard Murdoch, William Kendall, William Hartnell and Maya Koumani

programme.title: The Last Day of WW1
programme.desc: An account of how soldiers continued to be killed in battle hours after the armistice between the Allies and Germany had been signed in 1918. Presented by Michael Palin

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A bomb is found in a nightclub which German scientists claim cannot be disarmed, and Ash receives a mysterious message. Guest starring Judy Geeson (the original 1970s Poldark)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon spreads her wings and takes full advantage of her sister's hospitality, but Tracey is having money worries. Ross Kemp guest stars, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey makes an appointment with a Harley Street specialist, hoping to allay her suspicions that she may be pregnant. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: A postman is anxious to catch a morning showing of The Sound of Muzak at the local cinema. However, he is unaware a blue movie is actually to be shown and that his wife is its star. Comedy, written by and starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael throws a fancy dress party at his house to celebrate the 33rd anniversary of the film Zulu, but the mood turns sour when the Monopoly board comes out

programme.title: Long Weekend (1978)
programme.desc: A couple take a camping holiday at a remote beach to try and rebuild their struggling relationship. Their disrespect for the wildlife and the environment provokes the wrath of a supernatural presence, and the torment they endure from it brings tensions between them to the surface. Horror, starring John Hargreaves and Briony Behets

programme.title: Scramble (2017)
programme.desc: A man ends up in over his head in the world of organised crime when his girlfriend is kidnapped by an underground prostitution ring. His attempts to raise the ransom money land him a job delivering dead bodies for a crime syndicate. Crime thriller, starring Brett Newton and Victor Kelso

programme.title: The Harder They Come (1973)
programme.desc: A naive young man from rural Jamaica heads for Kingston in search of reggae stardom, only to get involved in the shady world of drug dealing as his musical career takes off. With no other option but to go on the run, he fights back against the system which forced him into a life of crime and becomes a political hero in the process. Crime drama, starring Jimmy Cliff, Janet Barkley, Carl Bradshaw and Bob Charlton

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Wapping for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks up The Great Orme in North Wales and beside a railway in West Yorkshire

programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Charlton Heston Presents the Bible
programme.desc: The renowned actor serves as storyteller an ancient Roman amphitheatre and other locales in Israel provide the settings, bringing the King James text to life

programme.title: The Chiltern Hundreds (1949)
programme.desc: An aristocrat fails to be elected as a Tory MP, so decides to stand for Labour instead _ only to learn his butler, determined to maintain class distinctions, is running against him. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, AE Matthews and David Tomlinson

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: A Stitch in Time (1963)
programme.desc: A butcher's bumbling assistant causes no end of accidents at work, the latest of which has landed his boss in hospital. While visiting, he meets a little girl who has not spoken since her parents died, and resolves to put a smile on the orphan's face. Slapstick comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Jerry Desmonde, Lucy Appleby and Jill Melford

programme.title: A Matter of Choice (1963)
programme.desc: A married woman finds herself caught in a moral dilemma when children inadvertently cause the death of her diabetic lover. Drama, starring Anthony Steel, Jeanne Moody and Ballard Berkeley

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike and Laura agree to go on a date, but chaos ensues in the restaurant when Laura loses a contact lens. Starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Laura buys a new dress for a special dinner with Ben. However, when her date reveals he is married, Mike happens by to pick up the pieces with his usual charm. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash gets some time off and plans to spend it in bed - but Susan has other ideas

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s. This edition focuses on the story of glam rock. Featuring David Bowie, Alice Cooper, Jethro Tull and Elton John

programme.title: Priest of Love (1981)
programme.desc: Christopher Miles's dramatisation of author DH Lawrence's years in the US, Mexico and England, charting events following the banning of his novel The Rainbow to the writing of Lady Chatterley's Lover. Starring Ian McKellen, Janet Suzman, Ava Gardner, Penelope Keith and John Gielgud

programme.title: The Statue (1971)
programme.desc: A linguist's wife sculpts a nude statue of him to celebrate his achievements. However, when she grows weary of his neglect, she reveals that its impressive physique was modelled on another man. Convinced she has taken a lover, he sets out to identify the man behind the sculpture. Comedy, starring David Niven, Virna Lisi and Robert Vaughn

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which 30 recurring players compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Teacher Sarah dreams of climbing Mount Kilimanjaro, but first she must take on the mighty Cube, while pizza delivery man Tony reckons he's got what it takes to beat it. Tackling a series of increasingly difficult challenges, the contestants are under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £250,000. Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kelly Cates, Adrian Chiles, Andy Hamilton, Kate Williams, Mark Dolan, Rustie Lee, Esther Rantzen and Tim Vine

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Chris, Helen, Pam and Steve pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Singer, TV presenter and 2010 Queen of the Jungle Stacey Solomon takes part in the game show opposite Emmerdale's Danny Miller (Aaron Livesy). The pair team up with their relatives to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, in which they can win up to £30,000 for their chosen charities. Vernon Kay hosts

programme.title: Fight Night
programme.desc: George Groves v Andrea Di Luisa. Another chance to see the bout for the vacant WBC International Super Middleweight title at the Copper Box in London. Home favourite Groves will have been hoping to bounce back immediately following his split-decision loss to Badou Jack for the WBC World title, which took place in September on the undercard of Floyd Mayweather Jr v Andre Berto in Las Vegas. A convincing win over Italian opponent Di Luisa would put Groves back on track for a fourth world title shot - potentially versus his old foe James DeGale, who holds the IBF belt
